preexisting_failures.cluster: cluster-C / MV-application-tracker-001 guard test — now MOOT, unrelated to MV-system-003
preexisting_failures.root_cause: Asserts 4 specific 'restored' prod Application row IDs (c0e3601826b6258afd1ced52d, c53d0b3ada038f7ce441dd018, c597b074bbd6214c31dcf75ec, ce01bad499189ac40e9e9c78f) exist in prod. Those rows were wiped by the very incident MV-system-003 remediates and have not been restored since (prod currently has 12 Application rows, none matching these 4 IDs per the failure assertion output). This is a follow-up defect in the cluster-C guard test itself (its 'restored rows present' assertion needs to be dropped/adjusted, keeping only the fixture-absence invariant) — separate finding, out of scope for MV-system-003, NOT a regression caused by this fix.

reasons: PASS - apps/api/tests/conftest.py:135-167 (_run_prod_truncate_guard + pytest_configure): session-start guard resolves the truncation DSN EXCLUSIVELY from DATABASE_URL_TEST (conftest.py:138, never DATABASE_URL), pins search_path via options= (conftest.py:82-89, mirrors app/db.py's own _translate_prisma_url), and verifies LIVE via SELECT current_schema() (conftest.py:115-132) before any fixture runs; any mismatch, missing DATABASE_URL_TEST, missing schema= param, or connection/query error is treated as unsafe and aborts the entire session via pytest.exit(returncode=2) (conftest.py:158-167) - fails closed on every error path, not just the expected one.

PASS - apps/api/tests/conftest.py:260-298 (_truncate_tables): rewritten to open its OWN connection via _resolve_truncation_dsn()/psycopg2.connect (never app.db.get_connection(), which reads whatever DATABASE_URL happens to be in the process env at call time) and re-verifies current_schema()=='aether_test' immediately before the TRUNCATE (conftest.py:277-279) - defense-in-depth independent of the session-start guard.

PASS - escape hatch AETHER_ALLOW_PROD_TRUNCATE (conftest.py:124, 141-143, 193, 277) requires an exact '1' string match, is documented as never-in-CI/deploy (DEPLOYMENT-RUNBOOK.md new S:0), and grep confirms it is NOT set anywhere in the current .env or either CI workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml, ci/github-actions-ci.yml).

PASS - scripts/run-tests.sh: never sources the repo-root .env wholesale (only greps the single DATABASE_URL_TEST line), refuses to run (exit 1) unless the resolved DSN literally carries schema=aether_test, and exports it as BOTH DATABASE_URL and DATABASE_URL_TEST for the pytest child - independently verified end-to-end (guarded-full-suite.txt).

PASS - DECISIVE PROOF: guarded full suite (695/700 passed) run against aether_test left prod aether byte-for-byte unchanged (User/Application/Job/StoryEntry/Resume/Subscription/ApprovalRequest all identical before vs after, independently re-queried by reviewer, not taken from the fixer's or coordinator's reports).

PASS - NEGATIVE CONTROL: pointing DATABASE_URL_TEST at the real production DSN caused pytest_configure's guard to abort with exit code 2 before any test ran and before any destructive SQL executed; prod counts confirmed unchanged immediately after.

NO PROHIBITED PATTERNS: no eslint-disable/ts-ignore (N/A, Python), no Math.random metrics, no TODO/FIXME/placeholder content reachable by users (test-infra only), the single '# noqa: BLE001' (conftest.py:149) is a scoped, justified blanket-except that converts ANY resolution/connection error into a LOUD ProdTruncationGuardError + pytest.exit (fail-closed), not a silent suppression - reviewed and accepted.

OBSERVATION (non-blocking): several other test files issue their own raw TRUNCATE/DROP TABLE via app.db.get_connection() rather than the new guarded helper - apps/api/tests/test_gap_p7_async_001.py:123, test_gap_p7_async_concurrency.py:50, test_agents_screen.py:32-33, test_provider_config.py:265, test_gap_p7_def_a_dual_mode.py:95. These remain safe ONLY transitively (they share the same DATABASE_URL env var that the session-start guard already verified equals the live-checked aether_test DSN, since conftest.py:52-54 sets DATABASE_URL=DATABASE_URL_TEST at import time before pytest_configure runs), not because they call the new guard functions directly. Not a blocker for MV-system-003 (which is scoped to the truncation path that caused the incident), but a maintenance risk worth a follow-up ticket if any future test opens a connection from a source other than DATABASE_URL/DATABASE_URL_TEST.

5 failures in the full-suite run are UNRELATED to this fix: 3 are pre-existing MV-system-002 credential-resolution failures caused by this VM's own CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN leaking into resolve_credential('anthropic') (verified independently by reading assertion output + grepping .env:49); 2 are now-moot cluster-C (MV-application-tracker-001) guard-test assertions expecting 4 specific prod Application rows that were wiped by the incident this very fix remediates and never restored - a separate follow-up item, not a regression from f13a256.
Branch fix/mv-system-003 confirmed NOT merged into main (git merge-base --is-ancestor f13a256 main fails) - fixer correctly left merge to the reviewer/coordinator per instructions.
recommendation: APPROVE MERGE of f13a256 for MV-system-003. Open two SEPARATE follow-up items (not blockers for this merge): (1) MV-system-002 CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN leak into resolve_credential during tests - likely needs the test suite to explicitly clear/monkeypatch that var; (2) cluster-C's test_mv_no_fixture_content_in_prod_data.py 'restored rows present' assertion is now unsatisfiable post-incident and needs updating to drop that assertion, keeping only the fixture-absence invariant.
